top_signals.title: Industrial Metals Climb as Traders Eye Hormuz and Fed Outlook
top_signals.summary: Copper and other base metals climbed as as traders awaited fresh peace talks between the US and Iran and clues on the outlook for US monetary policy.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Oil Prices Rise After US, Iran Attacks and Amidst Hopes of Hormuz Shipping Recovery
top_signals.summary: Oil prices gained more than 1% on Monday after attacks by the U.S. and Iran underscored the fragility of their interim peace deal, while cautious hopes of a continued recovery in energy shipping through the Strait of Hormuz limited gains.Iranian and U.S.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Asian Refiners Redirect Middle East Crude to the U.S. as Hormuz Flows Recover
top_signals.summary: Some Asian refiners have recently offered Middle Eastern cargoes to the U.S. West Coast as supply from the Persian Gulf rises with the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, while Asian buyers are well-supplied for the next two months. Refiners in Asia have spent the better part of the past four months scrambling to procure crude for the summer from producers outside the Middle East. Buyers now have enough non-Middle Eastern crude lined up to arrive over the next two months, meaning that spot purchases from the Middle East aren’t really an immediate…

top_signals.title: US Natural Gas Prices Fall With Ample Supply Maintaining Pressure on Market
top_signals.summary: U.S. natural gas futures fell for a second straight session on Monday, as ample supplies kept pressure on the market despite forecasts for hotter weather in the coming weeks. On its first day as the front-month, gas futures for August delivery on the New York Mercantile Exchange fell 8.2 cents…

top_signals.title: US racks up about 400 wins over illegal World Cup streaming sites
top_signals.summary: The World Cup’s organizing body, FIFA, helped identify hundreds of  domains taken down in an action organized by the U.S., along with the help of U.S. broadcaster NBC Universal and other entities.
